ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident was reported */,
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the route associated with the incident */,
  "incident_time" REAL /* Time when the incident occurred, represented as a fraction of a day */,
  "day_of_week" TEXT /* Day of the week when the incident occurred */,
  "location" TEXT /* Specific location of the incident */,
  "incident_type" INTEGER /* Type of incident (reference to incident_types) */,
  "min_delay" INTEGER /* Minimum delay caused by the incident in minutes */,
  "min_gap" INTEGER /* Minimum gap in service caused by the incident in minutes */,
  "direction" TEXT /* Direction of the route (e.g., SB for Southbound) */,
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vehicle involved in the incident */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the incident (e.g., Minor, Major, Critical) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the incident (e.g., Open, In Progress, Resolved) */,
  "resolution_time" REAL /* Time taken to resolve the incident in minutes */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Person or system that reported the incident */,
  "assigned_to" TEXT /* Team or individual assigned to resolve the incident */,
  "estimated_impact" INTEGER /* Estimated impact of the incident on service delivery in minutes */,
  "actual_impact" INTEGER /* Actual impact of the incident on service delivery in minutes */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the incident */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_route_id FOREIGN KEY ("route_id") REFERENCES routes ("route_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_day_of_week FOREIGN KEY ("day_of_week") REFERENCES days_of_week ("day_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_vehicle_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_id") REFERENCES vehicles ("vehicle_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_incident_type FOREIGN KEY ("incident_type") REFERENCES incident_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "routes" (
  "route_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each route */,
  "route_number" TEXT /* Route number */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the route */,
  "start_location" TEXT /* Starting location of the route */,
  "end_location" TEXT /* Ending location of the route */,
  "distance" REAL /* Total distance of the route in miles or kilometers */,
  "service_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of service (e.g., every 10 minutes, every 30 minutes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("route_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicles" (
  "vehicle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle */,
  "vehicle_number" TEXT /* Vehicle number */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of vehicle (e.g., Bus, Tram) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the vehicle */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the vehicle was manufactured */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_due" TEXT /* Date when the next maintenance is due */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the vehicle (e.g., In Service, Out of Service, Under Maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vehicle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the incident type (e.g., Mechanical, Investigation) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the incident type */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the incident type (1 being the least severe, 3 being the most severe) */,
  "impact_score" INTEGER /* Impact score of the incident type, used in calculations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "days_of_week" (
  "day_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each day */,
  "day_name" TEXT /* Name of the day (e.g., Sunday, Monday) */,
  "is_holiday" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the day is a holiday */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("day_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the incident associated with the log */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date when the log was created */,
  "log_details" TEXT /* Details about the log entry */,
  "logged_by" TEXT /* Person or system that created the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log entry (e.g., Initial Report, Update, Resolution) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)
